name: IP Printer
short: Stand in for up to 15 ESC/POS receipt printers on loopback IPs — receipts render on screen instead of paper.
lede: A Windows utility that impersonates up to fifteen ESC/POS receipt printers on loopback IPs. Point your POS at 127.0.0.1:9100, 127.0.0.2:9100, … and every receipt renders on screen — text, logos, QR codes — so you can verify print output without physical hardware.

name: SimCLI
short: Drive the Simphony client from the command line — log in, order items, press buttons, restart.
lede: A command-line interface for the Oracle Simphony POS client. It reads the live WPF element tree — not screenshots — so it can reliably log in, order items, press buttons and restart the client from a script or an AI agent.

name: Multi-KDS Display
short: Host many kitchen-display screens in one app — no more spinning up a VM per screen.
lede: Runs multiple Simphony KDS displays inside a single application, so you can bring up a whole kitchen of screens on one machine instead of one VM per display.

name: StsCLI
short: Command-line client for the Simphony Transaction Service API — authorize, list checks, post checks.
lede: A command-line interface for the Simphony Transaction Service (the Simphony API): authorize, list menu items, list open checks, post a check, and more — scriptable and agent-drivable.

name: Open Check Viewer
short: See open checks from the legacy Simphony API, in the raw Simphony XML format.
lede: Queries the legacy Simphony API and shows the open checks it returns in the raw Simphony XML format — handy for debugging integrations against what the API actually reports.

name: IqueryCLI
short: Query and export data from Oracle Simphony Reporting & Analytics on the command line.
lede: A command-line interface for querying and exporting data from Oracle Simphony Reporting & Analytics (iQuery) — scriptable, pipeable, and agent-drivable.
